scores.metric: full-build unmasked Levenshtein vs pristine text1b.o (pins + fill_delay rule neutralized)

sessions.lever: clean ctc2-wrapper, no pins/rule; canonical gate confirms ASM-PARTIAL

reviewer.evidence: GTE leaf wrapper (cop2 ctc2 body). Residual is register-rotation into $t0-$t4 (target uses HIGHER regs than GCC's ascending preference) + a jr-delay-slot fill — both non-pure-C, hand-asm hallmarks. Canonical-asm authorization is a user/orchestrator decision.

remaining_gap.summary: Target loads matrix[0..4] into $t0..$t4 (regs 8-12) then ctc2 to GTE $0-$4; GCC naturally packs into lower $v0/$v1/$a1/$a2 (ascending-reg preference) — opposite of target, no pure-C lever forces higher regs. Plus the final ctc2 zero,$7 must land in the jr-ra delay slot (fill_delay rule); GCC won't fill it.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 5 register-asm pins ($8-$12) + 1 fill_delay regfix rule — the canonical-asm wrapper form.

next_hypotheses: Authorize as COMPLETED-INLINE-ASM-CANONICAL (GTE leaf wrapper) per orchestrator/user policy — the documented endpoint for this class.
Cross-check the 2026-05-11 'Retired to pure C' siblings (func_80052A88/B7C/B00, func_8007ED6C) — verify they are genuinely COMPLETED-C and not carrying the same pin+fill_delay debt.

rejected_forms.form: clean plain-locals + ctc2 %0 constraints, no pins/rule
rejected_forms.reason: GCC picks lower regs ($v0/$v1/$a1/$a2) not target's $t0-$t4, and won't fill the jr delay slot — not a cheat, just non-matching; the gap is hand-asm register choice + delay fill.
